Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Proper Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two, And Set Two Form Interrogatory No. 17.1
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Garrett Sanderson III of CARROLL, BURDICK & McDONOUGH LLP (415-989-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.
Ted W. Pelletier of KAZAN, MCCLAIN, SATTERLEY & GREENWOOD (510-302-1000), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Harold Koepke and Nancy Karidis-Koepke

RULING:
DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED PROPER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET TWO, AND SET TWO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17.1

Defendant submitted to the Court's tentative ruling and the ruling was adopted: Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion to compel verified proper responses to requests for admission, set two, and set two form interrogatory no. 17.1 is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it properly met and conferred prior to filing this motion. (C.C.P. 2033.290(b), 2030.300(b), 2016.040.) Defendant violated the order made by this Court during the April 2, 2014 CMC that meet and confer needed to be conducted by actually talking to one another. Even ignoring that violation, defendant's alleged attempt to meet and confer did not demonstrate any willingness or intent to resolve the issue informally. Finally, this Court's order granting preference required that the parties have an informal discovery meeting with the Court prior to any discovery motion hearing, and defendant failed to have such meeting. Even if the motion was considered on its merits, it would be denied. Plaintiffs' responses to requests for admission nos. 32, 33, 37 and 38 are sufficient. Plaintiffs' amended responses to form interrogatories moot defendant's motion as to that issue. Subsequently the Court took the issue re: whether sanctions will be imposed under submission. A proposed order which tracked the tentative was submitted to the Court for consideration. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT BELL INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bell Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs' evidence creates a triable issue whether Mr. Koepke's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing mesothelioma. As to summary adjudication, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ted W. Pelletier of KAZAN, MCCLAIN, SATTERLEY & GREENWOOD (510-302-1000), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Harold Koepke and Nancy Karidis-Koepke

RULING:
DEFENDANT BELNORTEL CORPORATION, D.B.A. A.B.C. MOBILE BRAKE OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Belnortel Corporation, DBA A.B.C. Mobile Brake of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (punitive damages) and granted as to issues 2 and 3 (fraud/failure to warn and conspiracy to defraud/failure to warn). As to issue 1, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to issues 2 and 3, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable under plaintiff's fourth or sixth causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John W. Ranucci of LOMBARDI, LOPER & CONANT (510-433-2600) for defendant / moving party The Hertz Corporation
Ted W. Pelletier and Ian A. Rivamonte of KAZAN, MCCLAIN, SATTERLEY & GREENWOOD (510-302-1000), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Harold Koepke and Nancy Karidis-Koepke

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and the Court made the following rulings: Defendant The Hertz Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8, granted as to issue 2 (breach of implied warranty). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 4, 6, 7 and 8, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting these claims. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden as to these issues because it failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. As to summary adjudication issue 5, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. As to summary adjudication issue 2 (breach of implied warranty), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs' opposition did not oppose adjudication of this cause of action nor did plaintiffs submit admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable for breach of implied warranty. Subsequently the Court took summary adjudication as to issue 3 (strict liability) under submission. The Court received a proposed order by plaintiffs which track the Court's tentative ruling as to all other issues. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
